title: Thinking in Questions and Answers
summary: Explore how questions translate into SQL queries using the students_intro dataset.

sketchBlocks.title: Introduction to SQL Queries
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
SQL is a language used to interact with databases. It allows you to ask questions about the data stored in tables. For example, if you want to know the names of all students in a database, you can use a SQL query to retrieve that information. 

Here's a simple SQL query:

```
SELECT name FROM students;
```

This query selects the `name` column from the `students` table. Try it yourself by writing this query in the SQL editor and clicking 'Run query'.

sketchBlocks.title: Understanding the SELECT Statement
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown:
The `SELECT` statement is used to specify which columns you want to retrieve from a table. It is the most basic and essential part of any SQL query. 

For example, to get the names and grades of students, you can write:

```
SELECT name, grade FROM students;
```

This query will return a list of names and their corresponding grades. Try modifying the query to include the `age` column as well.

quizDraft.title: Basic SQL Query Structure
quizDraft.prompt: What is the purpose of the `SELECT` statement in SQL?
options: To delete data from a table
To retrieve data from a table
To update data in a table
To insert data into a table
quizDraft.hint: Think about what you want to do when you want to see data.

help.concept: The `SELECT` statement is used to retrieve data from one or more columns in a table.
help.hint_1: The `SELECT` statement is used to view data.
help.hint_2: It is the first part of a query that specifies which data you want to see.

quizDraft.title: Columns in a SQL Query
quizDraft.prompt: Which columns can you retrieve using the following query: `SELECT name, grade FROM students;`? Choose all that apply.
quizDraft.hint: Look at the columns listed after `SELECT`.

help.concept: The columns listed after `SELECT` are the ones that will be retrieved.
help.hint_1: Check the columns specified in the query.
help.hint_2: Only the columns explicitly mentioned will be retrieved.

quizDraft.title: Order of SQL Query Clauses
quizDraft.prompt: Arrange the SQL query clauses in the correct order.
quizDraft.hint: Consider the basic structure of a SQL query.

help.concept: A SQL query typically starts with `SELECT` followed by `FROM`.

quizDraft.title: Completing a SQL Query
quizDraft.prompt: Complete the SQL query to retrieve the `age` of students: `SELECT [blank1] FROM students;`
quizDraft.hint: Think about which column you want to retrieve.

help.concept: The column you want to retrieve should be placed after `SELECT`.
help.hint_1: You are interested in the `age` of students.

quizDraft.title: Retrieve Student Names
quizDraft.prompt: Write a SQL query to retrieve the names of all students from the `students` table.
quizDraft.hint: Use the `SELECT` statement to specify the column you want.

help.concept: To retrieve data, use the `SELECT` statement followed by the column name.
help.hint_1: You need to select the `name` column.
help.hint_2: The table you are querying is `students`.

quizDraft.title: SQL Query Result
quizDraft.prompt: What will be the result of the query `SELECT city FROM students;`?
quizDraft.hint: Look at the column specified after `SELECT`.

help.concept: The query retrieves the data from the column specified after `SELECT`.
help.hint_2: The result will be a list of cities.

quizDraft.title: Understanding SQL Syntax
quizDraft.prompt: Which of the following are valid SQL statements? Choose all that apply.
quizDraft.hint: Focus on statements that retrieve data.

help.concept: Valid SQL statements for this module involve selecting data.
help.hint_2: Statements that modify data are not covered in this module.

quizDraft.title: SQL Query Construction
quizDraft.prompt: Arrange the parts of a SQL query to form a correct statement.

help.concept: A SQL query starts with `SELECT` followed by `FROM`.

quizDraft.title: SQL Query Completion
quizDraft.prompt: Complete the SQL query to retrieve the `grade` of students: `SELECT [blank1] FROM students;`
quizDraft.hint: Think about which column you want to retrieve.

help.concept: The column you want to retrieve should be placed after `SELECT`.
help.hint_1: You are interested in the `grade` of students.

quizDraft.title: Retrieve Student Grades
quizDraft.prompt: Write a SQL query to retrieve the grades of all students from the `students` table.
quizDraft.hint: Use the `SELECT` statement to specify the column you want.

help.concept: To retrieve data, use the `SELECT` statement followed by the column name.
help.hint_1: You need to select the `grade` column.
help.hint_2: The table you are querying is `students`.

quizDraft.title: Retrieve Student Ages
quizDraft.prompt: Write a SQL query to retrieve the ages of all students from the `students` table.
quizDraft.hint: Use the `SELECT` statement to specify the column you want.

help.concept: To retrieve data, use the `SELECT` statement followed by the column name.
help.hint_1: You need to select the `age` column.
help.hint_2: The table you are querying is `students`.
